content:
type: STATUS
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Added _FILTERS registry + register_filter()/unregister_filter() in prompts.py; hooked via env.filters.update(_FILTERS) in _template_from_str and _template_from_file, applied BEFORE builtin filters so builtins always win. Since you're adding a create_jinja_env() helper with a local filters param, please call env.filters.update(_FILTERS) first thing inside it, then apply local filters after, so local overrides global as the spec requires. Let me know when your helper lands so I can adapt my hook into it instead of duplicating in both from_str/from_file.

content:
type: STATUS
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Finalized: _FILTERS registry + register_filter()/unregister_filter() in prompts.py, applied via env.filters.update(_FILTERS) in _template_from_str and _template_from_file BEFORE the builtin filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args) are set, so builtins always win over global. This doesn't touch your create_jinja_env/local-filters param directly since we're on separate sandboxes - if patches get merged, apply order should be: global registry, then builtins, then local filters last for local > builtin > global precedence (or adjust as needed). Tests pass, patch.txt written.

fields.summary: Finalized: _FILTERS registry + register_filter()/unregister_filter() in prompts.py, applied via env.filters.update(_FILTERS) in _template_from_str and _template_from_file BEFORE the builtin filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args) are set, so builtins always win over global. This doesn't touch your create_jinja_env/local-filters param directly since we're on separate sandboxes - if patches get merged, apply order should be: global registry, then builtins, then local filters last for local > builtin > global precedence (or adjust as needed). Tests pass, patch.txt written.
